'''Ben Hellewell''' (born 30 January 1992) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].

He was contracted to the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], playing on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Barrow Raiders]] in [[2012 Championship 1|Championship 1]]. He played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[Co-operative Championship]], and on loan from [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He has also played for the [[London Broncos]] in the Championship and in the [[Super League]], and on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Hellewell also played for the Leigh Centurions in the [[RFL Championship|second-tier]] and the Super League. Earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|ce|fb}} and {{rlp|wg}}er.

In 2019 he helped the Eagles to win the inaugural [[RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] as they defeated the [[Widnes Vikings]] 36–18 in the final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49458365|title=Sheffield Eagles beat Widnes Vikings 36–18 to win inaugural 1895 Cup|publisher=BBC Sport|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 September 2019}}</ref> He has earned 9 caps for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the European competitions and the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

==Background==
Hellewell was born in [[Bradford]], West Yorkshire, England.

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Hellewell started his career with the [[Bradford Bulls]], coming through the Bulls Academy at his hometown club.

===Warrington===
Hellewell moved to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] to begin his professional career. He won the Rugby League 9s tournament in June 2011 with Warrington.  In 2012 he was sent on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].  Hellewell spent part of 2012 on loan at the [[Barrow Raiders]] in [[2012 Championship 1|Championship 1]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Featherstone===
After failing to make a first team appearance for Warrington, he joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] in 2012, ahead of the [[2013 RFL Championship season]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Gordon|first=James|title=Featherstone snap up Ben Hellewell|url=https://loverugbyleague.com/news_10264-featherstone-snap-up-ben-hellewell.html|publisher=Love Rugby League|accessdate=26 October 2013|date=17 September 2012}}</ref> 
He played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[Co-operative Championship]], and on loan from [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[2014 Kingstone Press Championship]].

===London Broncos===
He joined the [[London Broncos]] ahead of the [[2015 RFL Championship season]]. 
[[File:Ben Hellewell.jpg|thumb|right|Hellewell playing for the London Broncos in 2016]]
In 2019 he spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. During that spell at the [[Sheffield Eagles|Eagles]] he helped the Eagles to win the inaugural [[RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] as they defeated [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] 36–18 in the final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49458365|title=Sheffield Eagles beat Widnes Vikings 36–18 to win inaugural 1895 Cup|publisher=BBC Sport|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 September 2019}}</ref>
In four years in the capital he played over 100 games, including gaining promotion to the [[Super League]].

===Leigh===
He joined [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] ahead of their [[2020 RFL Championship season]].

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 28 May 2022, Hellewell played for Featherstone in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final loss against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
In the 2023 Super League season, Hellewell played 17 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023}}</ref>

On 10 August 2025 he announced his immediate retirement from rugby league, needing a full hip replacement at just 33 years old.<ref>{{cite web|date=10 Aug 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|website=Love Rugby League|title=Salford Red Devils star announces immediate retirement as injury torment shared|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-star-announces-immediate-retirement-as-injury-torment-shared}}</ref>

==International==
Hellewell was a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international having made his début in 2011. He was named in their squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], and scored two tries in their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup Group C#Scotland vs Italy|30–30 draw]] with [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

In October and November 2014, he played in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]] competition. In October and November 2015, Ben played in the [[2015 European Cup]] competition. He was named in the Scotland squad for [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations]]. He was named in the Scotland squad for [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].
